title: 'In the war between the US and Iran, both sides are convinced they control the clock'
subhead: Tehran believes Trump needs a deal; Washington thinks the Islamic Republic cannot survive without one, explains Alain Frachon in his column for Le Monde.

title: Sarkozy hits back with new evidence at appeal trial over alleged Libyan funding
subhead: While the former French president was expected to react on Wednesday to a damning statement by his former associate, Claude Guéant, his lawyers instead submitted figures claiming that half of the sum the Libyans had sent was paid back to them.

title: Russia drastically scales back May 9 parade over fears of Ukrainian drone attacks
subhead: For the first time since 2008, the victory parade will feature no military hardware. In recent months, Ukraine has repeatedly carried out drone strikes deep inside Russia.

title: At the Musée Picasso Paris, Henry Taylor and the emergence of African American modernity
subhead: A hundred works by the artist reflect the influences that shaped part of his journey and capture, with intensity, the lives of individuals he encountered as well as the history of an entire country.

title: The French love their public libraries. But lack of resources undermines the experience
subhead: France's unique network of 15,500 local cultural spaces, which are open to all, free of charge, has seen visitor numbers steadily increase, leading to certain issues.

title: Were French people told that Chernobyl's cloud stopped at the border? A look back at a the making of a national myth
subhead: The legend of the radioactive cloud that supposedly never crossed into France has left a lasting mark on the national imagination. Yet that narrative has been rewritten many times over the decades.
